reviews.review_msg: I took long 280 with her lectures were super engaging and interesting, you can tell she cares about her students and she made the class fun and enjoyable to learn. Her exams were fair expect I found her quizzes were a bit more difficult than I expected but when the class told her it got fixed. Over all great, kind prof and would recommend

Marion is friendly, down-to-earth, and caring - one of my fav profs ever. I like how her quizzes (before remote learning) are taken individually followed by groups and she saves the highest mark. However, phonology can be a tricky subject with complicated rules. Do LOTS of practice questions and make sure you understand the answers thoroughly.

She is so nice. Always accessible outside class. Very careful in giving feedback and grading. Very patient in answering questions. Teaches very well. Although lots of hard work, she makes me interested in phonology. She is the kind of teacher that I will appreciate all the time.

Professor Caldecott was great. She always stops to make sure everyone understands and makes herself available to help despite her long commute. If I had one recommendation I would suggest posting lecture slides before class so students can read in advance because the textbook is sometimes tough to comprehend. But overall I enjoyed the class.

Marion is friendly and passionate about Linguistics but not great at articulating concepts in an easy to understand manner. The structure for the course didn't make sense because we were given small group problems before we even understood the material. This ended up wasting a lot of time that could have been spent learning how to actually do them.

Marion is a wonderful prof, she tries to relate to the students which is nice. There are weekly readings and a quiz on what you read. No midterm or final but weekly lab assignments and 3 lab papers throughout the year, leading to one BIG final paper. There was lots of options to boost your mark and everything was graded fairly. teaching @UVic
Caldecott was incredibly helpfull to me in my career pursuits and is an engaging lecturer. She is quite the character too.

Marion loves group work, which can be helpful or frustrating, depending on what kind of group you get into. Marion also loves being really sarcastic, which isn't for everyone. She's very much got the cheeky linguistic mum thing down. Marion's grading is fair, and she seems open to constructive criticism. I just don't think I'd take her again.

Really enjoyed her class - but definitely go over your marks against the answer key! Sometimes her multiple choice is poorly worded. That being said - She's very much willing to provide feedback if you ask. Very knowledgeable. I'll definitely take another course with her if the opportunity presents itself. Easy A if you're into speech science.

Grading is unclear.  provides a scale but actually curves the marks. exams are not representative of the material; easy questions get you same # of marks than hard ones. the most science-like ling course, yet, you will struggle as the material gets airy fairy.

Her teaching speed was fast and her lecture slides were not well-organized. There were quizzes for next week's materials. She makes you to read the textbook. I felt more confused after attending the lectures. If there were no group exams, my scores would be even lower.

I took LING221 and LING330 with Marion. Her lectures are easy to follow, clear and she's also pretty funny. There are weekly quizzes, homework assignments in addition to the exams. All three have a group component to them. She is super nice, approachable and takes the time to get to know her students. Her exams are tricky though, so do be prepared.

The course topics are very interesting , however, Dr. Caldecott makes seem material easier than what is actually is. The exams are not  well designed and lab questions sometimes  unclear or info scattered all over. She is very approachable and friendly but  sometimes  the only help I got was to be referred to the learning commons (3.5 CGPA tho)
Smart and fun with a very clear teaching style, which is hot in itself.  Smokin' prof!
Group exams are the best things ever ;)

She is very funny, helpful, and intelligent. Her explanation is clear! But content may be difficult. Keep up with materials. Then you'll be fine! Also, don't worry, Dr. Caldecott doesn't scale down. So your grade will be a bit higher than expected!! :)

Pay attention to marking, you will likely have to argue for marks as Dr. Caldecott uses a marker for most things; the marking was frequently unclear and not without errors, ie. things were sometime marked wrong that should not have been. Dr. Caldecott is an enthusiastic instructor but moves quickly through material.

I actually had Marion as my prof at UVic for a Phonetics/Phonology class. I had failed this class once before with a different prof and Marion changed everything. She helped me not only pass, but do VERY well grades-wise and now I actually enjoy the material. She is nice, funny, her assignments are clear and helpful and she has group midterms/final

she is a nice lady with passion all the time. her exams and homework are not easy. she likes to quiz on your previewing. it is easy to argue some points back from her if you can state your ideas. Your final grade will always HIGHER than your expectation, so don't worry too much in the middle of the semester if u feeling bad.

Marion is a wonderful teacher, she is super helpful and will always make time if you don't understand something. This isn't an easy course though, if you want to get a good mark you will have to work hard.
She is very kind. Since she just completed her PhD program, she is very motivated and hard working. She helps a lot if you try to learn. Exams are fair and she usually give you a good study guide.

She is a very nicer person, since she just completed her PhD program, she is very motivated and hard working. She helps a lot if you try to learn. Exams are not fair and she usually give you good study guide.

She's a nice teacher but strict. She's fair at marking but has ways to make you read the textbook. She can tell if you haven't read it b/c she'll answer your questions with a question that's suppose to "lead" you to the answer. She's nice, but exams are toughies. No final. i bombed the 2nd midterm and got a B :)
Difficult class. Horrible textbook. But Marion really cares about her students so she'll help you through.

She's new but she's a very nice person and her midterm exams aren't bad if you are good at memorizing things. Her questions on spectrogram analysis are crazy so make sure you know how to do those before you do the final.
